full_prompt: Design the frame assembly of a 2025 technical-college student-robotics competition robot. The robot must fit within an approximate 1 m^3 starting envelope (1000 mm x 1000 mm x 1000 mm). The per-team combined mass of all robots must be in the range 20-25 kg; this design targets a single robot of mass 22 kg. Pneumatic actuators, if used, must not exceed a gauge pressure of 0.5 MPa (500 kPa). Field obstacles include stair-like steps and ramps defined by the 2025 rulebook field CAD; the robot must be able to traverse at least a 50 mm step and a 15-degree incline. The frame must carry self-weight plus a 30 kg payload-carrying case (total 52 kg) under 2.5 g vertical equivalent shock with no permanent yield. The frame must also survive a 150 N horizontal impact applied at the top of the robot without visible deformation. Under the 2.5 g self-weight + payload case, the maximum deflection of the payload deck must not exceed 5 mm. The robot must satisfy a static tilt test of 30 degrees in any direction without tipping. Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for the two main load cases. The pneumatic pressure cap is a component-cert check. Traversal over the 50 mm step and 15-degree incline is a kinematic check combining wheel size, ground clearance, and CG, performed with CAD geometry (non-FEA). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the robot frame, a mass budget, and a report listing robot mass in kg, max von Mises stress per case in MPa, max payload deck deflection in mm, and tilt-to-tip angle in deg.
short_prompt:
Design the frame assembly of a 2025 technical-college student-robotics competition robot. Starting envelope ~1000 x 1000 x 1000 mm. Single-robot mass 22 kg (team combined 20-25 kg). Pneumatic actuators <= 0.5 MPa gauge (component-cert check). The robot must traverse a >= 50 mm step and a >= 15-degree incline (kinematic check) and remain stable on a 30-degree static tilt in any direction.

Load cases on the frame.
- LC1 self-weight plus 30 kg payload at 2.5 g vertical shock: (22+30) kg x 2.5 g (~1,275 N); payload-deck deflection <= 5 mm.
- LC2 top-of-robot horizontal impact: 150 N with no visible deformation.
- LC3 static tilt: 30 degrees without tipping.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Step and ramp traversal are CAD geometry checks, not FEA.

prompt.title: Technical-college student robot frame
prompt.brief: Design a 22 kg robot frame fitting 1 m^3, pneumatic ≤ 0.5 MPa, traverses 50 mm step and 15 deg incline, tilts to 30 deg stable.

load_cases.name: top_horizontal_impact
load_cases.description: horizontal force at top of robot
